Title: Tonoplast sucrose transporter SUT4-dependent sugar partitioning modulates phenological transitions and reproductive success in poplar

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon storage and partitioning; osmoprotection and stress protection for raffinose-family oligosaccharides during winter and reproductive development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Winter stem bark and xylem; floral buds/catkins
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SUT4 increases sucrose accumulation, alters winter raffinose/galactinol dynamics, accelerates autumn senescence, delays spring bud flush and cambial growth, reduces stem growth, and causes female sterility with disrupted catkin metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout in field-grown trees; seasonal metabolite profiling of bark/xylem and catkins by GC-MS; controlled pollination; RNA-seq of elongating catkins; phenology and growth measurements
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sucrose transporter family (SUT/SUC); tonoplast-localized sucrose transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed year-round with highest expression during cool seasons; predominant SUT expressed in poplar flowers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Winter-expressed; high in cool seasons and flowers/catkins
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field conditions across summer and winter seasonal transitions; cool season/winter dormancy; spring reproductive development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SUT4 is the main winter- and flower-expressed sucrose transporter examined in poplar. 2. CRISPR knockout of SUT4, but not SUT5/SUT6, caused higher summer stem sucrose, attenuated winter raffinose/galactinol accumulation, delayed spring growth, and earlier autumn senescence. 3. In catkins, SUT4 loss caused rising sucrose, abnormal raffinose trajectories, elevated stress-response transcripts, reduced proanthocyanidin-associated gene expression, and sterility.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper interprets SUT4 as mediating vacuolar sucrose efflux and cytosolic sucrose availability rather than total reserve abundance. Its metabolic effects are linked to sugar compartmentation, stress signaling, and circadian regulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced stem diameter and height, reduced wood specific gravity and modulus of elasticity, reproductive sterility
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for engineering seasonal carbohydrate allocation and reproductive traits in poplar, but knockout is detrimental; maintaining or fine-tuning SUT4 function is preferable to loss-of-function
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of elongating catkins, NCBI SRA SRP540970-SRP540981
